course_title: Veterinary Laboratory Diagnostics for Veterinary Technologists

learning_outcomes.description: Identify blood films, and haematologic and clinical chemistry data that require referral for pathological review.
Understand the format of diagnostic submission forms and advise on appropriate collection, preservation and packaging of specimens to optimise subsequent testing and diagnosis.
Describe the generic quality control that is fundamental to diagnostic laboratories and be able to explain the relationship between quality control and the usability of clinical diagnostic data.

Identify circumstances in which sample quality may influence testing and subsequent diagnostic reliability of haematological and biochemical data and be able to formulate contingency plans to counter adverse situations.
Acknowledge and manage the risks that accompany the submittal, processing and identification of infectious agents in a diagnostic laboratory.

assessments.task_description: A one hour online exercise, delivered in a standard Blackboard quiz format, requiring identification of leukocytes, assessment of red cell arrangement and morphology and platelet enumeration from provided digital images. The exercise can be accessed at any time during the date window, however it must be completed in a single sitting.
assessments.submission_guidelines: The Haematology Exercise can be accessed in the 'Assessment' section of the VETS3023 Blackboard site. This assessment item will be available after the gazetted opening date and can be undertaken and submitted at any time during the open period. The assessment item must be completed in a single sitting.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description: A 90 minute written examination canvassing ALL course material except for haematology.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.
